title: Mass Effect

notes: Unlike others, didn't require any tweaks to get audio working. Performance fine at 720p. After 3hrs gameplay, only encountered the odd minor graphical quirks with fixed black rectangles appearing when moving in specific locations: https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=1648234747
title: Mass Effect

notes:
No sound during gameplay.

The ini workaround causes crash immediately after character creation.

Start ME with Proton in debug mode using option PROTON_DUMP_DEBUG_COMMANDS=1 %command% ; run `/tmp/proton_*/run winecfg`; forced the use of the builtin openal32.dll.  Restart ME.

Sound works, no crashes w/ 3+ hours.
title: Mass Effect

notes: Ignore previous report of no audio tweak required, must have made the tweak after installing and forgot I made the change (see following https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/177). Played for 30+ hours, great performance generally apart from some minor audio distortion when some dialogues begin. The main issue is the semi-frequent "pure virtual function" crashes at seemingly random periods, could be 5 hours or 5 minutes until the next one. Otherwise, well worth playing and just remember to use quicksave regularly.
title: Mass Effect

notes: No problems after the audio fix described in https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/177

notes: Works good. Need to apply a fix for missing sound: https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/177#issuecomment-470393198 In rare cases can crash with "pure function call" error on loading a saved game. Just start it again and it will be fine.
title: Mass Effect

notes:
Had to apply the fix from https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/177#issue-353144749 to get sound, now everything works perfectly.

Sometimes the mouse gets stuck after alt-tabbing out of the game. Entering the settings menu fixes this.
title: Mass Effect

notes: The audio works fine with setting openal32=native in winecfg. Presumably the config file tweak mentioned in other reports here and elsewhere also works. However, the game has immersion-breaking FPS slowdowns on my machine at the galaxy map in the very beginning and at the beacon at the very first mission (and presumably here and there later in the game, but haven't played further). Reducing details does not fix / alleviate the issue. This is a known issue in the game (even on plain Windows) but Proton exacerbates the issue for some reason on my setup. I haven't found a workaround, and the issue is so sever I must only give a Bronze rating. This slowdown does *not* happen in plain Wine nearly as severely (it is there, but to a much lesser extent).
title: Mass Effect

notes: Regression in 4.2, won't even launch. Worked fine for me (barring the occasional "pure virtual function call" crash) in Proton v3.16.
title: Mass Effect

notes: Audio tweak required (set openal32=builtin - not native!) or the config file tweak linked here. Also, csmt hurts performance, reducing FPS to less than half at some parts of the game. Run winecfg on the overlay and disable csmt, and FPS should be quite OK after that!
title: Mass Effect

notes: No longer launches. Stuck at "Preparing to launch". Waited several minutes. This is not the normal "upgrading to new Proton version" type delay.
title: Mass Effect

notes: Runs, but often crashes with the well-known (even on Windows) "Pure virtual function called" error. Happens rather randomly: You might be able to play for hours or only a few minutes or seconds before hitting it. Needs "openal32.dll" to be set to "Built-in" (not native!) or you won't have any sound.
title: Mass Effect

notes:
When I launched the game there was no sound. The hardware sound is enabled by default which causes the issue. 
To fix the problem the following lines had to be added to the Mass Effect/BioGame/Config/DefaultEngine.ini file, under the ISACTAudio.ISACTAudioDevice section:
DeviceName=Generic Software
UseEffectsProcessing=False

After I restarted the game the audio worked fine. 

The gameplay itself is smooth and I had no issues. It runs well on my older hardware too. Hopefully this audio issue will be fixed in some upcoming patches.
title: Mass Effect

notes:
To fix the sound problem the following lines had to be added to the Mass Effect/BioGame/Config/DefaultEngine.ini file, under the ISACTAudio.ISACTAudioDevice section: DeviceName=Generic Software UseEffectsProcessing=False .

After that the game runs  perfectly for me. But it is gold because it will need a few tweaks for the best performance and experience apart from the sound working. Some graphical uplifts are possible too. Runs like it does in Windows once the sound patch. Little effort.

notes:

in the ISACTAudio.ISACTAudioDevice section add these two lines:

DeviceName=Generic Software

title: Mass Effect

notes:
Runs butter smooth - just needs the following lines to BIOEngine.ini in the ISACTAudio.ISACTAudioDevice section to get audio working:

DeviceName=Generic Software 
UseEffectsProcessing=False
title: Mass Effect

notes: Disable Esync helps preventing a crush after exiting a video. As others reported, you need to edit "Mass Effect/BioGame/Config/DefaultEngine.ini" and add  the following two lines to the section starts with "[ISACTAudio.ISACTAudioDevice]": DeviceName=Generic Software", "UseEffectsProcessing=False" (without the quotes).
title: Mass Effect

notes: 1) In the Launch Options set PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE=1 PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command%, this will allow the game to use more than 4GB of RAM, and helps prevent crashes after exiting a video. 2) Go "%HOME%/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Mass Effect/" 3) Open the "BioGame/Config/" directory, and open DefaultEngine.ini, find the section called "[ISACTAudio.ISACTAudioDevice]", and add "DeviceName=Generic Software" without quotes, and add "UseEffectsProcessing=False" without quotes, to enable sound. If you are experiencing lag during gameplay, in the game directory, as mentioned before, open the directory "/Mass Effect/Enging/Config/", then open "BaseEditor.ini" and under the section called "[CurveEd]", change the value of "MaxViewRange" by deleting some of the zeros before the decimal point.
title: Mass Effect

notes:
I set the launch options to PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE=1 PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command%

%HOME%/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Mass Effect/BioGame/Config/DefaultEngine.ini" file.

Add the following entries under [ISACTAudio.ISACTAudioDevice] to enable sound.

DeviceName=Generic Software
UseEffectsProcessing=False

title: Mass Effect

notes:
Optimus laptop so added primusrun to beginning of command.

Dunno if it's necessary but I did this as well: PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE=1

Then edited the DefaultEngine.ini file. Added:
DeviceName=Generic Software
UseEffectsProcessing=False

MAKE SURE you have the space between Generic & Software. Won't work otherwise.

If you want it in 1080p, in the same file, DefaultEngine.ini: find the section titled: [WinDrv.WindowsClient]
And changed the started up resolution for x and y to be 1920 and 1080 respectively. Seems to make it a bit grainy.

title: Mass Effect

notes: Edit DefaultEngine.ini to enable sound. just because title-screen sound works doesn't mean game sound will work. Also edited file to change screen size to 2560x1440. Was black screen until then. Added force big address tweak also. (Launch Options: noesync forcelgadd nod3d10 )
title: Mass Effect

notes:
My launch options (I have optimus, so ignore the primusrun unless you have a discrete GPU): PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE=1 PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 primusrun %command%

 ---- With the tweaks listed in other reports: Look for DefaultEngine.ini file (in .steam/steam/steamapps/common/Mass Effect/BioGame/Config/), open it and look for [ISACTAudio.ISACTAudioDevice] section. Add:

DeviceName=Generic Software

and then

UseEffectsProcessing=False

----- Make sure there is a space between "Generic" and "Software."

If you want to up the resolution you'll find the startup resolution x and y in the same file. (The game is meant to look grainy. You can turn it off in the settings).

Save and restart the game.

title: Mass Effect

notes:
Before the audio fix (disabling processing and changing the name of the audio device in game's INI files), it would crash constantly.

Those with an AMD CPU (!) that is somewhat new, the game relies on 3DNow! technology in a few places and that is not included on AMD's CPUs anymore, so you might get black-blobby characters. For me, the console fix didn't work, so instead I changed my CPU Vendor's name in the wineprefix's regedit from AuthenticAMD to Null (could be whatever, I suppose).
title: Mass Effect

notes: After applying audio fix.
title: Mass Effect

notes:
To fix the sound issue, rename "Mass Effect/Binaries/OpenAL32.dll" to something else, e.g. "_OpenAL32.dll".
For performance and stability improvements, use D9VK and enable Large Address Aware.
title: Mass Effect
